title: Baked Rigatoni with Spinach and Fresh Mozzarella
ingredients: 2 cans (28 ounce each) plum tomatoes in tomato puree, preferably San Marzano
¼ cup coarsely chopped fresh basil
1 pound spinach, stemmed, well rinsed but not dried and coarsely chopped
1 pound fresh mozzarella cheese, cut into ½ inch cubes
½ cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese plus more for serving
instructions: Warm 2 tbsp EVOO in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced garlic and stir until pale gold, about 1 minute.
Add the canned plum tomatoes and cook until the sauce thickens, about 30 minutes.
Using a potato masher, break up the tomatoes into chunky pieces.
Stir in the basil, kosher salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes. Keep warm.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
Lightly oil a 13x9 inch baking pan with olive oil.
Heat 2 tbsp EVOO in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook until aromatic, about 1 minute.
In batches, stir in the spinach, letting each batch wilt before adding the next.
Remove the spinach mixture from the heat.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil over high heat.
Add the rigatoni pasta and cook according to package directions until very al dente.
Drain the pasta well and return to the pot.
Add the tomato sauce, spinach mixture, and ¾ of the mozzarella cubes to the pasta and mix well.
Season generously with salt and pepper.
Transfer the mixture to the prepared baking pan.
Top with the remaining mozzarella cubes and Parmesan cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until heated through and cheese is melted.
notes: Use San Marzano tomatoes for best flavor
Spinach should be well rinsed but not dried before adding to allow residual moisture to help cook it
Cook pasta until very al dente since it will finish cooking in the oven
Fresh mozzarella cubes add creaminess to the dish
